metadata.title: Bizarre Lineage Tier List - Practical Meta Guide
metadata.description: A practical Bizarre Lineage tier list page built around PvP pressure, PvE value, consistency, and how much investment a stand needs before it feels good.

hero.title: Bizarre Lineage Tier List built for judgment, not noise
hero.description: The goal of this page is to explain why a stand belongs in a tier, not just throw labels at the reader. Rankings should stay tied to real use cases and be revised after balance changes.

introduce.title: How to read this MVP tier list
introduce.description: Treat the page as a framework for ranking decisions. Early entries can be conservative until more in-game validation is added.

items.title: S Tier
items.description: Reserved for options that stay strong across multiple scenarios instead of dominating only one niche.

items.title: A Tier
items.description: Reliable stands with strong upside, but with clearer tradeoffs in matchup spread, execution, or farming comfort.

items.title: B Tier
items.description: Usable choices that can work well in the right hands, but usually need more commitment or specific context.

items.title: Sample anchor
items.description: Star Platinum is linked as the first stand sample page so readers can move from ranking to detailed judgment.

benefits.title: Ranking dimensions that matter
benefits.description: Each tier decision should be defensible. If a page cannot explain the criteria, the ranking is not useful.

items.title: PvP pressure
items.description: How often the stand can force mistakes, convert openings, and stay threatening in common fights.

items.title: PvE value
items.description: How comfortable the stand feels while clearing routine content, grinding progress, or handling bosses.

items.title: Investment efficiency
items.description: How much time, matchup knowledge, or supporting setup is needed before the stand starts paying off.

features.title: MVP ranking notes
features.description: These notes define the structure the full tier table should follow as more stands are added.

items.title: Lead with the quick conclusion
items.description: Put the highest-level takeaway near the top so players do not have to scroll through justification before seeing the result.

items.title: Link each highlighted stand to a deeper page
items.description: The tier list should act as a decision hub, not a dead end.

items.title: Separate PvP and PvE comments when they diverge
items.description: Some stands are much better in one mode than the other, and the page should say that plainly.

items.title: Update tiers after balance shifts
items.description: A tier list without version-awareness becomes stale fast, especially for Roblox games with active tuning.

items.title: Keep unsupported claims out
items.description: If the page cannot verify a detail, label it as provisional instead of presenting it as settled fact.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Common questions about how to use the ranking page.

items.question: Does one tier list work for both PvP and PvE?
items.answer: It can, but only if the page explains when those judgments split. If a stand is excellent in one mode and average in the other, the write-up should say so directly.

items.question: Why is the tier list still conservative?
items.answer: This is an MVP template. It favors defensible structure and linked sample pages before scaling to a full roster.

items.question: What should a reader open after the tier list?
items.answer: A detailed stand page such as Star Platinum, or the Beginner Guide if the player still needs a first-session route.

cta.title: Need a stand-level breakdown?
cta.description: Open the first stand sample page to see how the wiki turns a ranking into a clearer investment decision.
